libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker service or external Jackrabbit/WebDAV server is needed. Prefer in-process unit tests over network integration tests: instantiate request/response/context/method classes directly and use small HttpServletRequest/HttpServletResponse stubs where servlet-facing behavior is exercised. Use the legacy javax.servlet API, not jakarta.servlet. No special environment variables or system properties were found to be required.

deterministic_setup.reason:
The library exposes and implements servlet-based classes such as AbstractWebdavServlet, WebdavRequestImpl, DavServletRequest/DavServletResponse, InputContextImpl, and OutputContextImpl, while the artifact POM marks servlet-api as provided; tests that exercise these APIs need the old javax.servlet AP
...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8851 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.apache.jackrabbit:jackrabbit-webdav:2.0-beta4; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: If servlet-api is omitted, tests covering servlet/server/request/response classes may fail to compile or load even though the library dependency resolves.
Avoid adding a full Jackrabbit webapp/JCR repository or live WebDAV server setup; the library documentation describes this artifact as interfaces and common utility classes, not a deployable server.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: jackrabbit-webdav is an in-process WebDAV server/client utility library, but its servlet-facing APIs are meaningful coverage targets and the resolved artifact declares javax.servlet:servlet-api as a provided dependency, so generated tests need that API added explicitly.
